title: parashorea macrophylla
intro: Parashorea macrophylla, also known as the Borneo ironwood or Belian, is a large tree species native to the tropical forests of Borneo and Sumatra. It is valued for its timber, which is highly prized for its durability and resistance to rot and insects. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against certain diseases. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Parashorea macrophylla are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to provide a range of health benefits and support overall well-being.
